item.title: Kids free at Northwest Trek
item.description:
Sunday, March 1, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Northwest%20Trek%20Wildlife%20Park%2C%2011610%20Trek%20Dr%20E%2C%20Eatonville%2C%20WA%2098328

Eatonville (54 miles S) Each paying adult can bring two children for free (age 12 or younger) during Kids & Critters Weekends at Northwest Trek Wildlife Park, 11610 Trek Drive E in Eatonville. Valid on Friday, Saturday, and Sunday.

Event details

item.title: Beer week
item.description:
Sunday, March 1, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Tacoma

Bars and breweries host 55 beer-tasting and food-pairing events during Tacoma Beer Week.

Event details

item.title: Short films
item.description:
Sunday, March 1, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Seattle & Shoreline

Watch some of the Oscar Nominated Short Films nominated for this year’s Academy Awards, grouped by category (animated, documentary, or live action), at two venues. Landmark Crest, Shoreline SIFF Cinema Uptown, Seattle

Event details

item.title: Cirque show
item.description:
Sunday, March 1, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Marymoor%20Park%2C%206046%20West%20Lake%20Sammamish%20Pkwy%20NE%2C%20Redmond%2C%20WA%2098052

Redmond (16 miles NE) A female heroine discovers that her choices can influence the world as she progresses through the stages of evolution in Echo, performed by Cirque du Soleil in a giant heated tent at Marymoor Park, 6046 West Lake Sammamish Pkwy NE in Redmond.

Event details

item.title: Dramatic gay opera
item.description:
Sunday, March 1, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=McCaw%20Hall%2C%20321%20Mercer%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1.2 miles NW) Set in the nation’s capital, Fellow Travelers is “heart-wrenching” [NY Times] historical fiction about two men in a relationship who must make impossible choices as they struggle to survive the Lavender Scare of the 1950s at McCaw Hall, 321 Mercer St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Sherlock Holmes comedy
item.description:
Sunday, March 1, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Everett%20Performing%20Arts%20Center%2C%202710%20Wetmore%20Ave%2C%20Everett%2C%20WA%2098201

Everett (29 miles N) Baskerville is a “madcap sendup” of Sir Arthur Conan Doyle’s classic whodunit, a fast-paced adventure full of clues, silly accents, disguises, and deceit at Everett Performing Arts Center, 2710 Wetmore Ave. in Everett.

Event details

item.title: Live: The Notebook
item.description:
Tuesday, March 3, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=The%20Paramount%20Theatre%2C%20911%20Pine%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.3 miles NE) Based on the novel and popular film, The Notebook tells the story of a couple who share a lifetime of love despite forces that threaten to pull them apart at The Paramount Theatre, 911 Pine St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Comicon
item.description:
Thursday, March 5, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Seattle%20Convention%20Center%2C%20705%20Pike%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.2 miles E) Emerald City Comic Con is a giant “comics, anime, gaming, entertainment, and pop culture event” that encourages attendees to wear costumes. Located at Seattle Convention Center, 705 Pike St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Kids’ art day
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Kent%20Commons%2C%20525%204th%20Ave%20N%2C%20Kent%2C%20WA%2098030

Kent (20 miles SE) Kids create hands-on art projects for one flat fee of $10 at Kent Kids’ Arts Day in Kent Commons, 525 4th Ave. N in Kent. Visit the food truck or bring your own lunch. Adults and children age 2 and younger are free.

Event details

item.title: International family festival
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Marcus%20Pavilion%20At%20Saint%20Martin's%20University%2C%205300%20Pacific%20Ave%20SE%2C%20Lacey%2C%20WA%2098503

Lacey (58 miles S) Lacey Cultural Celebration has music and dance performances, demonstrations, displays, food and craft vendors, and kids’ crafts in the Marcus Pavilion at Saint Martin's University, 5300 Pacific Ave. SE in Olympia. No pets.

Event details

item.title: Dollhouse & miniature show
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=DoubleTree%20Suites%20Southcenter%2C%2016500%20Southcenter%20Pkwy%2C%20Tukwila%2C%20WA%2098188

Tukwila (14 miles SE) The $15 Seattle Miniature Show has exhibits, 40 vendors, and workshops at DoubleTree Suites Southcenter, 16500 Southcenter Pkwy in Tukwila. Children younger than age 12 are free.

Event details

item.title: Lunar New Year — Chinatown
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Hing%20Hay%20Park%2C%20423%20Maynard%20Ave%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098104

International District (1.2 miles S) Join the Lunar New Year Celebration in Chinatown, which includes lion and dragon dances, martial arts demonstrations, arts & crafts, and a food walk that has 40 destinations surrounding Hing Hay Park, 423 Maynard Ave. S in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Women’s Day lunch
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Edmonds%20Waterfront%20Center%2C%20220%20Railroad%20Ave%2C%20Edmonds%2C%20WA%2098020

Edmonds (17 miles N) A $75 ticket to Edmonds International Women’s Day includes lunch, a speaker, two breakout sessions, and a happy hour at Edmonds Waterfront Center, 220 Railroad Ave. in Edmonds for age 13+.

Event details

item.title: Remodeling expo
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Tacoma%20Convention%20Center%2C%201500%20Commerce%20St%20%2C%20Tacoma%2C%20WA%2098402

Tacoma (34 miles S) Get ideas for your dream home from speakers, vendors, and remodeling experts at Tacoma Remodeling Expo at Tacoma Convention Center, 1500 Commerce St. in Tacoma. Children younger than age 18 are free.

Event details

item.title: Wine tasting at Lake Chelan
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=%2C%20Chelan%2C%20WA%2098166

Chelan (180 miles E) A $78 ticket to Taste Chelan includes “a curated taste of food & wine at four locations,” with a choice of four different itineraries centered around the city of Chelan, WA. Transportation is not included.

Event details

item.title: Small-town wine tasting
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Thomas%20Family%20Farm%2C%209010%20Marsh%20Rd%2C%20Snohomish%2C%20WA%2098296

Snohomish (28 miles NE) Snohomish Wine Festival features 20 wineries, with beer, cider, spirits, kombucha, and optional food at Thomas Family Farm, 9010 Marsh Road in Snohomish for age 21+. Your $45 ticket includes a glass, 8 tastes, and a $5 food voucher. No children or pets.

Event details

item.title: Mussel festival
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Coupeville%20Wharf%2C%2024%20Front%20St%20NW%2C%20Coupeville%2C%20WA%2098239

Coupeville (58 miles NW via ferry) Penn Cove MusselFest offers food vendors, cooking demonstrations, mussel farm tours by boat ($22, $6 child), chowder tasting ($54), a beer & wine garden, mussel-eating competitions, and kids’ activities at Coupeville Wharf, 24 Front St. NW in Coupeville on Whidbey Island. The $54 Mussel Mingle for adults is Fri. & Sat. evening.

Event details

item.title: Hot-air balloons
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Methow%20Valley%20Thriftway%2C%20920%20State%20Rte%2020%2C%20Winthrop%2C%20WA%2098862

Winthrop (240 miles NE) If the weather cooperates, watch a dozen hot-air balloons inflate and launch each morning (7 to 9 a.m.) at Winthrop Balloon Roundup. Park at Methow Valley Thriftway, 920 State Rte 20 in Winthrop. The Saturday night glow (5:30 p.m.) is on Riverside Ave. No pets or smoking.

Event details

item.title: Bourbon tasting
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Daniel%E2%80%99s%20Broiler%2C%2010500%20NE%208th%20St%2C%20Bellevue%2C%20WA%2098004

Bellevue (10 miles E) Sample dozens of bourbons with heavy appetizers, and admire the view from the 21st floor, at $263 Bellevue Bourbon Bash at Daniel’s Broiler, 10500 NE 8th St. in Bellevue. Bring age 21+ ID.

Event details

item.title: Dance festival
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Erickson%20Theatre%2C%201524%20Harvard%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098122

Capitol Hill (0.9 miles NE) Seattle International Dance Festival (SIDF) Winter Mini-Fest features works by Khambatta Dance Company of Seattle plus Colectivo Glovo of Spain and Portugal, onstage at Erickson Theatre, 1524 Harvard Ave. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Modern African dance
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=The%205th%20Avenue%20Theatre%2C%201308%205th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.2 miles SE) Award-winning Camille A. Brown & Dancers perform a new work called I am, featuring dance genres of the African Diaspora and creating a creative space for cultural liberation where anything is possible at The 5th Avenue Theatre, 1308 5th Ave. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Erotic art party
item.description:
Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Gallery%20Erato%2C%20309%201st%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098104

Pioneer Square (1 mile S) Creative dress is welcome at $130 A Black Tie Affair, which has performances, live music, snacks, a no-host bar, and an art preview to benefit the Erotic Art Festival (held in May) at Gallery Erato, 309 1st Ave. in Seattle for age 21+.

Event details

item.title: CD & record sale
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Seattle%20Center%20Armory%2C%20305%20Harrison%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1 mile NW) “Find tons of collectible records, CDs, and other music-related collectibles for sale” on 50 tables during Northwest Record Show at Seattle Center Armory, 305 Harrison St. in Seattle. Early admission at 8 a.m. costs more. Held in spring and fall. Poster.

item.title: Soup tasting
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=National%20Nordic%20Museum%2C%202655%20NW%20Market%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098107

Ballard (5.5 miles NW) A $10 donation online gets you five tastes of soup from around the world at Seattle Sister Cities’ Soup Sampling at National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Irish show
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Kenyon%20Hall%2C%207904%2035th%20Ave%20SW%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%209812

Fauntleroy (8.3 miles SW) Magical Strings performs “ancient airs and their own unique compositions — on hand-crafted Celtic harps and hammered dulcimers along with pennywhistles, button accordion, and concertina” with a fiddler and Irish dancers at Kenyon Hall, 7904 35th Ave. SW in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Color festival – Redmond
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Redmond%20City%20Hall%20Campus%2C%2015670%20NE%2085th%20St%2C%20Redmond%2C%20WA%2098052

Redmond (15 miles NE) Attended by 10,000 people, Festival of Color has music, dancers, food vendors, and colored powder flying through the air at Redmond City Hall Campus, 15670 NE 85th St. in Redmond. Admission and parking are free, but there is a cost to buy colored powder.

Event details

item.title: State park day
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: All state parks

Event details

item.title: Wrestling
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 4:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Climate%20Pledge%20Arena%2C%20334%201st%20Ave%20N%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1.2 miles NW) Watch the mega personalities of professional wrestling square off in the ring during WWE Monday Night Raw at Climate Pledge Arena, 334 1st Ave. N in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Party for young professionals
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Nordstrom%20Southcenter%2C%20100%20Southcenter%20Mall%2C%20Tukwila%2C%20WA%2098188

Bellevue (11 miles E) Happy Hour & Hellos includes beer, wine, mocktails, appetizers, and networking at Terry's Kitchen, 5625 119th Ave. SE in Bellevue. Click their RSVP button for a $10 ticket. Repeats several times a year.

Event details

item.title: Fascinating stories
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Benaroya%20Hall%2C%20200%20University%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.3 miles SW) Hear a dozen speakers, each talking 5 minutes about an interesting subject while projecting photos at Ignite Seattle at Benaroya Hall, 200 University St. in Seattle. Doors open 6:30 p.m.

Event details

item.title: RV show
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Lumen%20Field%20Event%20Center%2C%20800%20Occidental%20Ave%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098134

SoDo (1.5 miles S) See hundreds of new recreational vehicles and accessories at Seattle RV Show, which also has seminars, and a beer & wine area at Lumen Field Event Center, 800 Occidental Ave. S in Seattle. Children age 17 and younger are free.

Event details

item.title: Burlesque circus
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 9 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=The%20Triple%20Door%2C%20216%20Union%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.3 miles S) “A seductive circus comes to town with amazing aerialists, drag clowns, burlesque lions, and more” when Kat Robichaud presents Misfit Cabaret: Circus at The Triple Door, 216 Union St. in Seattle for age 17+ (9 p.m. 21+).

Event details

item.title: Quilt show
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Evergreen%20State%20Fairgrounds%2C%2014405%20179th%20Ave%20SE%2C%20Monroe%2C%20WA%2098272

Monroe (33 miles NE) Admire 400 quilts, see demonstrations, and shop from vendors at $15 Quilters Anonymous Annual Quilt Show at Evergreen State Fairgrounds, 14405 179th Ave. SE in Monroe. Children age 12 and younger are free.

Event details

item.title: Golf show
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Washington%20State%20Fair%20Events%20Center%2C%20110%209th%20Ave%20SW%2C%20Puyallup%2C%20WA%2098371

Puyallup (37 miles S) Seattle Golf Show has 150 exhibitors, free lessons, a junior golfer area, games with prizes, stage presentations, and a bar at Washington State Fair Events Center, 110 9th Ave. SW in Puyallup. Children age 12 and younger are free.

Event details

item.title: Dinner dance party
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Pickering%20Barn%2C%201730%2010th%20Ave%20NW%2C%20Issaquah%2C%20WA%2098027

Issaquah (16 miles E) A $70 ticket to Beats, Barrels and Brews [formerly Boots, Barrels and Brews] includes 3 drink tickets, a catered buffet dinner, dessert, dancing, games, and a band playing the hits of Abba in Pickering Barn, 1730 10th Ave. NW in Issaquah. Bring age 21+ ID.

Event details

item.title: Color festival – Bellevue
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Crossroads%20Park%2C%2016140%20NE%208th%20St%2C%20Bellevue%2C%20WA%2098008

Bellevue (14 miles E) Welcome spring at the colorful Hindu festival of Holi, with DJ dance music, Indian food for sale, and colored powder you can buy and throw at Crossroads Park, 16140 NE 8th St. in Bellevue.

Event details

item.title: St Patrick’s bar crawl
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Pioneer Square (multiple locations)

Wear a costume or something green to Seattle LepreCon. Your ticket includes a costume contest, drink specials, and no cover charge at eight bars near Pioneer Square for age 21+. State law does not allow you to take drinks outside bars.

Event details

item.title: St. Patrick’s run — Tacoma
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=The%20Forum%2C%20815%20Pacific%20Ave%20A%2C%20Tacoma%2C%20WA%2098402

Tacoma (34 miles S) Wear green on the St. Paddy’s Day Run, a scenic 5K run/walk, plus 10K and half-marathon running events, which begin outside The Forum, 815 Pacific Ave. A in Tacoma. No pets.

Event details

item.title: St. Patrick’s — Eastside
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=McMenamins%20Anderson%20School%2C%2018607%20Bothell%20Way%20NE%2C%20Bothell%2C%20WA%2098011

Bothell (20 miles NE) St. Patrick’s Day Celebration promises food & drink specials, a roaming bagpiper, live music until 10 p.m., and “plenty of hijinks” at McMenamins Anderson School, 18607 Bothell Way NE in Bothell. Free parking.

Event details

item.title: St. Patrick’s parade
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 12:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Pier%2066%2C%202225%20Alaskan%20Way%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Seattle waterfront St. Patrick’s Day Parade includes Irish bands, bagpipers, dancers, marchers, floats, and antique cars. The 2026 parade on Alaskan Way goes from Pier 66 to Pier 58.

Event details

item.title: Wine tasting in Bothell
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=First%20%26%20Main%20Design%20Market%2C%2010100%20Main%20St%2C%20Bothell%2C%20WA%2098011

Bothell (20 miles NE) A ticket to Shamrock Shuffle Wine, Beer & Spirits Walk includes 10 tastes from Washington wineries & breweries in retail shops, beginning at First & Main Design Market, 10100 Main St. in Bothell for age 21+. Bring a glass and wear green. Only $117 VIP tickets are still available.

Event details

item.title: Jewish films
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Mercer Island & Seattle

Seattle Jewish Film Festival celebrates Jewish culture, filmmaking, and history. Special events include an opening-night dessert party (March 14), a brunch (March 15), shorts (March 18), guest speakers on several dates, and a closing reception (March 29). Held in Seattle theaters and at Stroum Jewish Community Center on Mercer Island. Brochure.

Event details

item.title: Balkan music & dance
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=St.%20Demetrios%20Greek%20Orthodox%20Church%2C%202100%20Boyer%20Ave%20E%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098112

Montlake (3.7 miles NE) Balkan Night Northwest features multiple bands, dancing, performances, and food and drink for sale at St. Demetrios Greek Orthodox Church, 2100 Boyer Ave. E in Seattle. Children younger than age 15 are free. [The Balkan countries are located between Italy and Turkey.]

Event details

item.title: Irish show
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Edmonds%20Center%20for%20the%20Arts%2C%20410%204th%20Ave%20N%2C%20Edmonds%2C%20WA%2098020

Edmonds (18 miles N) A Celtic Celebration features “Celtic fiddle tunes, Irish songs and ballads, and Irish step dancing” with Celtic folk group The Gothard Sisters at Edmonds Center for the Arts, 410 4th Ave. N in Edmonds.

Event details

item.title: Irish Festival
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Seattle%20Center%20Armory%2C%20305%20Harrison%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1 mile NW) Irish music, dancing, films, exhibits, vendors, and children’s activities await you at the Irish Festival in Seattle Center Armory, 305 Harrison St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: St. Patrick’s run — Seattle
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Seattle%20Center%2C%20305%20Harrison%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Lower Queen Anne (0.8 miles NW) Wear green or a costume to the St. Patrick’s Day Dash 5K at Seattle Center. Afterward, head to the giant beer garden at Fisher Pavilion for age 21+. The Leprechaun Lap is a 1K dash for kids age 10 and younger.

Event details

item.title: Irish films
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Pacific%20Science%20Center%2C%20200%202nd%20Ave%20N%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1 mile NW) Emerald City Irish Film Festival screens seven Irish films plus a program of short films in the Imax theater at Pacific Science Center, 200 2nd Ave. N in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Tacoma Oscar party
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Grand%20Cinema%2C%20606%20Fawcett%20Ave%2C%20Tacoma%2C%20WA%2098402

Tacoma (35 miles S) The Awards Watch Party [formerly Film Awards Party] features a red carpet, drinks & snacks for sale, costume contests, a competition to guess the winners, and at 4 p.m. a live broadcast of the Academy Awards onscreen at Grand Cinema, 606 Fawcett Ave. in Tacoma. Click “4:00 PM” on their page then “Continue as guest.”

Event details

item.title: Birding festival
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Pavilion%20at%20the%20Blaine%20Community%20Center%2C%20635%208th%20St%2C%20Blaine%2C%20WA%2098230

Blaine (110 miles N) Wings Over Water Northwest Birding Festival celebrates migratory birds that flock to the pristine coastal area near Blaine and has expert speakers, exhibits, and kids’ activities. Some field trips are not free. Shop from vendors in the Pavilion at the Blaine Community Center, 635 8th St. in Blaine. Schedule.

Event details

item.title: St. Patrick’s — Pike Place
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Kells%20Irish%20Restaurant%20%26%20Pub%2C%201916%20Post%20Alley%20Walk%20Wy%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.3 miles SW) Loud and crowded St. Patrick's Irish Festival delivers an authentic pub experience, with live Irish music and classic Irish fare at Kells Irish Restaurant & Pub, 1916 Post Alley in Seattle for age 21+. Tickets $24.

Event details

item.title: St. Patrick’s — Ballard
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Conor%20Byrne%20Pub%2C%205140%20Ballard%20Ave%20NW%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098107

Ballard (4.9 miles NW) Celebrate St. Patrick’s Day with live music until 11 p.m. at Conor Byrne Pub, 5140 Ballard Ave. NW in Seattle for age 21+. Irish dancers at 1:30 & 7:30 p.m. They say tickets are $30 at the box office.

Event details

item.title: St. Patrick’s — Tacoma
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=McMenamins%20Elks%20Temple%2C%20565%20Broadway%2C%20Tacoma%2C%20WA%2098402

Tacoma (34 miles S) St. Patrick’s Day Celebration promises food & drink specials, a pipe band at 6 p.m., live music until 10 p.m., and “plenty of hijinks” at McMenamins Elks Temple, 565 Broadway in Tacoma.

Event details

item.title: State park day
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: All state parks

Event details

item.title: Gourmet party
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=The%20Admiral's%20House%2C%202500%20W%20Marina%20Pl%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098199

Interbay (3.1 miles NW) A $203 ticket to Pacific Standard includes “all of the food and beverage you like at this walk around event” with seafood and wine at The Admiral's House, 2500 W Marina Place in Seattle for age 21+. This event is part of Taste Washington.

Event details

item.title: Seafood at the ocean
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Ocean%20Shores%20Visitor%20Information%20Center%2C%20120%20W%20Chance%20a%20La%20Mer%20NW%2C%20Ocean%20Shores%2C%20WA%2098569

Ocean Shores (132 miles SW) Razor Clam & Seafood Festival has clam chowder, a raw oyster bar, Dungeness crab dinners, adult beverages, live music, Crab Benedict (Sun. reserve online), and vendors selling clams, scallops, fish & chips, salmon, burgers, and funnel cakes at Ocean Shores Visitor Information Center, 120 W Chance a La Mer NW in Ocean Shores.

Event details

item.title: Wine party
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Lotte%20Hotel%2C%20811%205th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098104

Downtown (0.5 miles S) Dress up and taste wine, drink signature cocktails, meet winemakers, eat gourmet appetizers, play retro video games, dance to DJ music, and hear musicians at $174 The New Vintage at Lotte Hotel, 811 5th Ave. in Seattle. Must be age 21+. This event is part of Taste Washington.

Event details

item.title: Ballet
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=McCaw%20Hall%2C%20321%20Mercer%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1.2 miles NW) Pacific Northwest Ballet performs Firebird about a beautiful, mythical bird that comes to the aid of two lovers after a sorcerer casts a cruel spell, plus two other works at McCaw Hall, 321 Mercer St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Modern dance
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Paramount%20Theatre%2C%20911%20Pine%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.3 miles NE) Award-winning Alvin Ailey American Dance Theater performs thought-provoking new works and perennial favorites at Paramount Theatre, 911 Pine St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Brain festival
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Pacific%20Science%20Center%2C%20200%202nd%20Ave%20N%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1 mile NW) BrainFest includes shows, talks, and exhibitions about the human brain at Pacific Science Center, 200 2nd Ave. N in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Women’s films
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=SIFF%20Film%20Center%2C%20305%20Harrison%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1.4 miles NW) Watch 24 “woman-centric” short films from around the world during Post Alley Film Festival at SIFF Film Center, 305 Harrison St. in Seattle. The last two hours are a reception. All day admission ranges from $20 to $32.

Event details

item.title: Taste of Georgetown
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Star%20Brass%20Works%20Lounge%2C%205813%20Airport%20Way%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098108

Georgetown (5.1 miles S) Buy $5 tickets for Georgetown Bites in the parking lot of Star Brass Works Lounge (5813 Airport Way South), and then exchange the tickets for bites and beverages at 35 participating businesses.

Event details

item.title: Color festival – Phinney
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Phinney%20Center%2C%206532%20Phinney%20Ave%20N%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098103

Phinney Ridge (5.1 miles N) Celebrate the traditional Hindu festival at Holi Celebration with entertainment, food vendors, and colored powder flying through the air at Phinney Center, 6532 Phinney Ave. N in Seattle. Adult $20, child $8.

Event details

item.title: Night market
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Columbia%20City%20Night%20Market%2C%204850%2037th%20Ave%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098118

Columbia City (5.5 miles SE) Monthly Columbia City Night Market has 8 food trucks, 20 makers and vendors, live music, and a beer garden on 37th Avenue S. (from S. Ferdinand to S. Edmunds). Walk 10 minutes from Columbia City rail station to 4850 37th Ave. S in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: French festival
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Seattle%20Center%20Armory%2C%20305%20Harrison%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1 mile NW) French Fest is a celebration of French-speaking culture, with art, live music, dancing, films, wine tasting, a baguette tasting contest, vendors, and sustainability workshops, both upstairs and on the main floor of the Seattle Center Armory, 305 Harrison St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Wine tasting event
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Lumen%20Field%20Event%20Center%2C%20800%20Occidental%20Ave%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098134

SoDo (1.5 miles S) Taste Washington events are for age 21+. Sat. — Wine Seminars are held at Embassy Suites Pioneer Square. Sat. & Sun. — The Grand Tasting features chef demos and samples from 200 wineries and 75 restaurants at Lumen Field Event Center, 800 Occidental Ave. S in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: World explorer show
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Benaroya%20Hall%2C%20200%20University%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.3 miles SW) National Geographic Live presents scientists, photographers, filmmakers, and adventurers live on stage with amazing photos and video at Benaroya Hall, 200 University St. in Seattle. March is about the Galapagos.

Event details

item.title: Country dance weekend
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Renaissance%20Seattle%20Hotel%2C%20515%20Madison%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098104

Downtown (0.5 miles SE) Emerald City Hoedown is an LGBTQ friendly weekend of dancing, with 24 workshops and lots of social events at Renaissance Seattle Hotel, 515 Madison St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Films by young directors
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=SIFF%20Cinema%20Uptown%2C%20511%20Queen%20Anne%20Ave%20N%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Lower Queen Anne (1.5 miles NW) National Film Festival for Talented Youth (NFFTY) showcases the best directors age 24 and younger, with a focus on “young persons from traditionally marginalized communities” at SIFF Cinema Uptown, 511 Queen Anne Ave. N in Seattle. Opening night March 26 begins with a red carpet and ends with an after-party at SIFF Cinema Downtown, 2100 4th Ave. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Jazz bands
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=The%20Paramount%20Theatre%2C%20911%20Pine%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.3 miles NE) Top jazz bands from five local high schools wow the audience at $29 Hot Java Cool Jazz in The Paramount Theatre, 911 Pine St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Party at the art museum
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Seattle%20Art%20Museum%2C%201300%201st%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.4 miles SW) A $65 ticket to SAM Remix includes “innovative performances, art-making activities, live music, and gallery admission” with snacks for sale and a no-host bar at Seattle Art Museum, 1300 1st Ave. in Seattle for age 21+.

Event details

item.title: Outdoor festival
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Lake%20Sammamish%20State%20Park%2C%202000%20NW%20Sammamish%20Rd%2C%20Issaquah%2C%20WA%2098027

Issaquah (15 miles SE) Spring into Adventure has art, gardening, and health vendors at Lake Sammamish State Park, 2000 NW Sammamish Road in Issaquah. Parking is $10 if you don’t have a Discover Pass. Register online if you want a $6 class in yoga, wand making, forest bathing, or workout.

Event details

item.title: Color festival – Seattle Center
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Mural%20Amphitheater%2C%20305%20Harrison%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (0.8 miles NW) Wear clothes you don’t mind staining to Seattle Color Festival, which has food trucks, DJ music, colored powder for sale, and dancing to celebrate Indian Holi at Seattle Center in the Mural Amphitheater, 305 Harrison St. in Seattle. [Note that the No Kings protest march will arrive at Seattle Center about 2 p.m.]

Event details

item.title: Polish food & bazaar
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Polish%20Cultural%20Center%2C%201714%2018th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098122

Capitol Hill (1.8 miles NE) Eat pierogi, sausage, cabbage rolls, borscht, beer, coffee, and pastries downstairs (or buy food to go), and then browse Polish crafts for sale upstairs at the Polish Spring Bazaar at the Polish Cultural Center, 1714 18th Ave. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Ballard wine walk
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Columbia%20Bank%2C%205512%2022nd%20Ave%20NW%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098107

Ballard (5.1 miles NW) Get 10 tastes in 15 Ballard shops that host pop-up tasting rooms featuring Washington wines during $45 Ballard Wine Walk for age 21+. Check in at Columbia Bank, 5512 22nd Ave. NW in Seattle. Bring a glass or pay extra. Held in March and October.

Event details

item.title: Ballet for families
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 3:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=McCaw%20Hall%2C%20321%20Mercer%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1.2 miles NW) Based on a Japanese folk tale, Momotaro is the story of a young boy who befriends animals, fights ogres, and seeks treasures for his family, performed by ballet students at McCaw Hall, 321 Mercer St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Scottish bagpipes & dance
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Washington%20State%20Fair%20Events%20Center%2C%20110%209th%20Ave%20SW%2C%20Puyallup%2C%20WA%2098371

Puyallup (37 miles S) Listen to Scottish pipes and drums, wear the tartan of a Scottish clan, and join Scottish Highland dancing at the Tartan Ball at Washington State Fair Events Center, 110 9th Ave. SW in Puyallup. In previous years, music began at 6:45 p.m. “Feel free to bring your own food for yourself or to share with your table.”

Event details

item.title: Dance show
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Renton%20IKEA%20Performing%20Arts%20Center%2C%20400%20S%202nd%20St%2C%20Renton%2C%20WA%2098057

Renton (13 miles SE) Watch dance performances (ballet, flamenco, and more) by participating dance schools during $28 Evergreen Dance Festival at Renton IKEA Performing Arts Center, 400 S 2nd St. in Renton.

Event details

item.title: Fashion show
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=415%20Westlake%20Ave%20N%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

South Lake Union (0.8 miles N) “Fashion designers present their most inspired masterpiece creations” during $82 Metropolitan Fashion Week Seattle at 415 Westlake Ave. N in Seattle for age 13+. Cash bar and after-party.

Event details

item.title: Taste of Rainier Valley
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Rainier Valley (6 miles S)

Plate of Nations offers special deals on international meals. Click any of the 50 restaurants to see prices.

Event details

item.title: Baseball
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=T-Mobile%20Park%2C%201250%201st%20Ave%20S%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098134

SoDo (1.6 miles S)

Event details

item.title: Underground tour
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Beneath%20the%20Streets%2C%20102%20Cherry%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098104

Pioneer Square (0.8 miles S) Step beneath modern Seattle to explore the original street level, which was buried after The Great Fire of 1889. Your $30 Underground History Tour begins at Beneath the Streets, 102 Cherry St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Free things to do
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 12 PM

Event details

item.title: Variety show
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Broadway%20Performance%20Hall%2C%201625%20Broadway%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098122

Capitol Hill (0.8 miles NE) Billed as the world’s largest comedy/variety festival, Moisture Festival presents 30 shows, mostly at Broadway Performance Hall, 1625 Broadway in Seattle. Friday shows are for age 18+.

Event details

item.title: Tulip festival
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Mount%20Vernon%20Downtown%20Association%2C%20223%20S%201st%20Street%2C%20Mount%20Vernon%2C%20WA%2098273

Mount Vernon (61 miles N) Check if flowers are blooming before you go. April 1 - 30 — Skagit Valley Tulip Festival features spectacular fields of blooming tulips and daffodils. Some fields are not free. April 11 (noon) — Tulip parade in La Conner. April 17 - 19 — Tulip Festival Street Fair is near Mount Vernon Downtown Association, 223 S 1st Street in Mount Vernon.

Event details

item.title: All-American musical
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Village%20Theatre%2C%20303%20Front%20St%20N%2C%20Issaquah%2C%20WA%2098027

Issaquah (17 miles E) Take a rollicking trip back to the 1950s with the cast of Grease as they navigate the social waters of high school and love at Village Theatre, 303 Front St. N in Issaquah. Repeats later in Everett.

Event details

item.title: Live theater
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 12 PM

Event details

item.title: Museum day
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Seattle & Tacoma

item.title: Swing dance
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Hyatt%20Regency%20Bellevue%2C%20900%20Bellevue%20Way%20NE%2C%20Bellevue%2C%20WA%2098004

Bellevue (9.2 miles E) Easter Swing is an annual weekend of competitions, workshops, and social dances at Hyatt Regency Bellevue, 900 Bellevue Way NE in Bellevue.

Event details

item.title: Science fiction & fantasy convention
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=DoubleTree%20Hotel%20Seattle%20Airport%2C%2018740%20International%20Blvd%20%2C%20SeaTac%2C%20WA%2098188

SeaTac (15 miles S) Norwescon features 200 vendors, panelists, events, game playing, dances, and a masquerade contest at DoubleTree Hotel Seattle Airport, 18740 International Blvd. in SeaTac.

Event details

item.title: Hockey
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Climate%20Pledge%20Arena%2C%20334%201st%20Ave%20N%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Seattle Center (1.2 miles NW)

Event details

item.title: Art walks
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: multiple locations

Event details

item.title: Australian cirque show
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Meany%20Center%20for%20the%20Performing%20Arts%2C%204040%20George%20Washington%20Lane%20NE%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098195

University District (4.6 miles NE) Circa is a 10-member cirque troupe that combines amazing acrobatics, the grace and athleticism of ballet, and story-based theatrics at Meany Center for the Performing Arts, 4040 George Washington Lane NE in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Easter train
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Chehalis, Elbe, Everett, Snoqualmie

April 3 — Everett Railroad April 3, 4, 5 — Mt. Rainier Scenic Railroad April 4 — Chehalis Central Railroad April 4, 5 — Snoqualmie (no Easter theme)

Event details

item.title: Anime festival
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Seattle%20Convention%20Center%2C%20705%20Pike%20St%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

Downtown (0.2 miles E) Anime is a style of Japanese film and TV animation featured at Sakura-Con, plus gaming, cosplay, cultural panels, dances, concerts, exhibits, and industry guests at Seattle Convention Center, 705 Pike St. in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Student art show
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=9th%20Ave%20Gallery%2C%202014%209th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098121

Downtown (0.5 miles N) Cornish College of the Arts showcases the work of its Bachelor of Fine Arts students at the Art BFA Showcase at 9th Ave Gallery, 2014 9th Ave. in Seattle. Free reception the first day (April 3, 6 to 9 p.m.).

Event details

item.title: Rugby
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Starfire%20Sports%2C%2014800%20Starfire%20Way%2C%20Tukwila%2C%20WA%2098188

Tukwila (12 miles S)

Event details

item.title: Small-town Easter parade
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=First%20Street%2C%201st%20St%2C%20Snohomish%2C%20WA%2098290

Snohomish (29 miles NE) Billed as the state’s only Easter parade, Snohomish Easter Parade includes floats, bands, kids, and pets on First Street in Snohomish, followed by an Easter bonnet contest.

item.title: Family music
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Town%20Hall%20Seattle%2C%201119%208th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098101

First Hill (0.5 miles SE) Entertaining for both parents and children, The Brian Waite Band combines musical theater, imaginative storytelling, laughs, and original rock ‘n roll songs at Town Hall Seattle, 1119 8th Ave. in Seattle. Check out the free tickets for age 22 and younger.

Event details

item.title: Tea party at the longhouse
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Duwamish%20Longhouse%2C%204705%20W%20Marginal%20Way%20SW%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098106

North Delridge (7.9 miles S) The $70 Princess Angeline Spring Tea usually features a speaker plus “tea and light refreshments” at the Duwamish Longhouse, 4705 W Marginal Way SW in Seattle. Princess Angeline was the eldest daughter of Chief Seattle. No children or pets.

Event details

item.title: Bunny bar crawl
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Downtown Seattle

Wear bunny ears or a costume, and then hop from bar to bar during BunnyCon. Your ticket includes a wristband, live entertainment, and drink specials for age 21+.

Event details

item.title: Daffodil parade
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Pierce County

The Grand Floral Parade includes daffodil-covered floats, marching bands, clowns, and pirates that travel through Tacoma (10:15 a.m.), Puyallup (12:45 p.m.), Sumner (2:30 p.m.), and Orting (5 p.m.). It’s the highlight of the Daffodil Festival.

Event details

item.title: Easter egg hunts
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: multiple locations

Kids Out & About Seattle’s Child Tacoma (Pierce County) Olympia (Thurston County) Washington state by county

Event details

item.title: Gourmet cooking class
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Hotel%20Andra%2C%202000%204th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098121

Belltown (0.1 miles NW)

Event details

item.title: Everett Film Festival
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 12:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Everett%20Performing%20Arts%20Center%2C%202710%20Wetmore%20Ave%2C%20Everett%2C%20WA%2098201

Everett (29 miles N) Everett Film Festival focuses on films by or about women at Everett Performing Arts Center, 2710 Wetmore Ave. in Everett.

Event details

item.title: Comedy showcase
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Washington%20Hall%2C%20153%2014th%20Ave%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098122

Central District (1.5 miles SE) Hear seven of Seattle’s “most innovative comics” tell their best jokes at Undisputable Champions of Comedy at Washington Hall, 153 14th Ave. in Seattle for age 21+.

Event details

item.title: Easter cruise
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=Waterways%20Cruises%2C%20901%20Fairview%20Ave%20N%2C%20Seattle%2C%20WA%2098109

Eastlake (1.3 miles N) Enjoy a $155 Easter brunch cruise that includes a champagne or apple juice toast, a brunch buffet, and two hours of lake views from your private table, departing from Waterways Cruises, 901 Fairview Ave. N in Seattle.

Event details

item.title: Easter restaurants
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: multiple locations

See recommended restaurants for Easter brunch or dinner. Gayot Guide — Seattle, Everett, Snoqualmie Open Table — Seattle, Eastside, Tacoma Seattle Met — Seattle, Kenmore, Renton Seattle’s Child — Seattle, Seatac, Eastside Yelp — Seattle, Eastside, Tacoma

Event details

item.title: Music by candlelight
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: multiple locations

Event details
